 {"id":"bd-fi65","title":"Implement basic mode TUI (gum-based fallback)","description":"Task: Implement Basic Mode TUI (gum-based)

Purpose
-------
Provide a simple but functional TUI when advanced dashboard not available,
using gum for styled output and selection.

When Used
---------
- gum available but not full TUI mode
- User prefers simpler interface
- Debugging/development

Implementation
--------------

show_question_basic_mode()
  Display single question with gum styling:
  
  gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" \
    --border-foreground "#fab387" \
    "Question from: $repo"
  
  echo "$question"
  
  Build options array from JSON
  gum choose "${option_labels[@]}"

show_discovery_summary_basic()
  gum style --border rounded "Discovery Summary"
  echo "Repos with activity: $count"
  echo "Total work items: $items"
  echo "High priority: $high"

show_session_status_basic()
  Simple text status:
  for session in sessions:
    echo "$repo: $state"

basic_mode_loop()
  Sequential question processing:
  while questions pending:
    show_question_basic_mode
    answer = gum choose
    send_answer
    mark_answered

gum_confirm_wrapper()
  with fallback to read prompt:
  
  if GUM_AVAILABLE:
    gum confirm "$prompt"
  else:
    read -rp "$prompt [Y/n] " yn
    case "${yn,,}" in y|yes) return 0 ;; esac

Progress Display
----------------
gum spin --title "Processing..." -- command
gum style --foreground "green" "✓ Done"

Formatting Helpers
------------------
format_priority_badge()
  case priority:
    CRITICAL: gum style --foreground "red" "CRITICAL"
    HIGH: gum style --foreground "orange" "HIGH"
    NORMAL: gum style --foreground "yellow" "NORMAL"
    LOW: gum style --foreground "gray" "LOW"

ANSI Fallback
-------------
If gum not available, use ANSI codes:
- Bold: \033[1m
- Colors: \033[31m (red), etc.
- Box drawing: Unicode ─│┌┐└┘

Testing
-------
- Verify gum styling renders
- Verify choice selection works
- Verify ANSI fallback works
- Test without gum installed

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Questions display with styling
- [ ] Options selectable via gum choose
- [ ] Progress shown with gum spin
- [ ] Works without gum (ANSI fallback)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:50:49.453778878-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:50:49.453778878-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented basic TUI question loop with gum/ANSI fallback","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fi65","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:13.195307516-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-g7gw","title":"E2E: Comprehensive logging infrastructure","description":"## Objective
Implement and test detailed JSON logging across all E2E tests.

## Requirements

### Log Format Standard
```json
{
  "timestamp": "ISO8601",
  "test_name": "string",
  "phase": "setup|execute|verify|cleanup",
  "operation": "string",
  "duration_ms": number,
  "result": "pass|fail|skip",
  "context": { /* operation-specific data */ },
  "error": { "type": "string", "message": "string", "stack": "string" }
}
```

### Features to Implement
1. Structured JSON log writer for test framework
2. Log aggregation across test phases
3. Summary report generation
4. Log filtering by severity/phase
5. Machine-readable test results
6. Human-readable formatted output

### Integration Points
- test_framework.sh logging functions
- Per-test log files in $TEST_LOG_DIR
- Aggregate report in TAP + JSON formats

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All E2E tests emit structured JSON logs
- [ ] Log viewer/filter tool works
- [ ] Summary reports generated automatically
- [ ] Logs capture full context for debugging failures","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:57:28.077846221-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:57:28.077846221-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-g7gw","depends_on_id":"bd-6crg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:57:38.630406765-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gfd4","title":"Installer: remove set -e and echo -e","description":"install.sh currently uses global 'set -euo pipefail' and relies on 'echo -e' for colored logging; AGENTS.md requests no global set -e and best practice prefers printf. Make install.sh explicit-error-handling (set -uo pipefail), switch logging to printf, and avoid Bash-4-only lowercase expansions in prompts for broader portability.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-04T19:43:24.045649031-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:45:32.845850452-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:45:32.845850452-05:00","close_reason":"Remove global set -e, switch logging to printf, add explicit error handling, avoid bash-4-only lowercase expansion"}

 {"id":"bd-sv6v","title":"Implement retry with exponential backoff","description":"Retry with exponential backoff for transient failures.

Function: retry_with_backoff(cmd, max_attempts, base_delay)

Algorithm:
- Start with base_delay (default 1s)
- On failure: delay = base * 2^(attempt-1)
- Add jitter: +/- 25% randomization
- Continue until success or max_attempts

Use cases:
- API calls that timeout
- Rate limit recovery
- Network glitches

Implementation logs warnings on each retry, returns final exit code.

Acceptance: Works for common failure patterns, respects max attempts, jitter prevents thundering herd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:23.01899538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","close_reason":"Added retry_with_backoff (exp backoff + jitter) and used for gh api GraphQL/rate_limit; fixed governor grep portability","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv6v","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:15.633158875-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
